task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application window is open, and the title bar, xsheet, or scene browser indicates that 'dwanko_run.tnz' is the active loaded scene.

task_completion.description: Add a new text level to the scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz xsheet or timeline shows a new column/layer created for text, or the text tool is actively selected and being used in the main viewer.

task_completion.description: The text content added to the scene is exactly 'EPISODE 1'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'EPISODE 1' is clearly typed and visible within the text tool bounding box or rendered in the main viewer.

task_completion.description: The text 'EPISODE 1' is centered and visible in the scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'EPISODE 1' is positioned approximately in the center of the camera view/canvas and is large or contrasting enough to be clearly visible against the background.

task_completion.description: Configure the render settings to output a PNG sequence to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings window in OpenToonz is open, showing the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/title_card/' and the file format set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Render the first 10 frames as a PNG sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows a sequence of PNG files (between 8 and 12 files) successfully created in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/title_card/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to edit the scene and render the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenToonz interface (menus, timeline, viewer, output settings) rather than using terminal commands or scripts to manipulate the .tnz file directly.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually edit the .tnz XML/text file to add the text level.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) open displaying the raw code/XML contents of the 'dwanko_run.tnz' file.

integrity.description: The output PNG files were generated by OpenToonz.
integrity.visual_evidence: The rendering progress bar or output settings in OpenToonz correspond to the final files shown in the output directory, rather than files being copied or downloaded via terminal.
